title: Reading Kosher Symbols: OU, OK, Star-K and What They Mean
title_zh: 认识犹太洁食认证标志：OU、OK、Star-K 及其含义
title_he: קריאת סימני כשרות: OU, OK, Star-K ומה הם אומרים
description: A comprehensive guide to understanding kosher certification symbols including OU, OK, Star-K, and other major hechsherim, with explanations of letter codes (D, P, M), supervision processes, and practical guidance for reading food labels.
description_zh: 全面指南，帮助理解洁食认证符号，包括 OU、OK、Star-K 和其他主要 hechsherim，解释字母代码（D、P、M）、监督流程以及阅读食品标签的实用指导。
description_he: מדריך מקיף להבנת סימני הכשר כולל OU, OK, Star-K והכשרים מרכזיים אחרים, עם הסברים על קודי אותיות (D, P, M), תהליכי פיקוח והדרכה מעשית לקריאת תוויות מזון.
target_audience: Beginners to kosher observance, converts, and anyone learning to navigate kosher food shopping
target_audience_zh: 洁食遵守的初学者、皈依者以及任何学习浏览洁食购物的人
target_audience_he: מתחילים בשמירת כשרות, גרים, וכל מי שלומד לנווט בקניית מזון כשר
author: Torah Light Editorial Team
cultural_parallels: Comparison to Chinese traditional food preparation standards and trust in local craftsmen
Parallels to traditional Chinese medicine ingredient sourcing and preparation standards
Reference to 食不厌精 (food preparation refinement) and 时令而食 (eating seasonally)
topics_covered: Major kosher certification agencies (OU, OK, Star-K, Kof-K, CRC)
Understanding letter codes (D for dairy, P for pareve, M for meat, Fish)
How kosher supervision works (mashgiach, ingredient approval, equipment koshering)
Special considerations (Cholov Yisroel, Yoshon, Bishul Yisroel, Pat Yisroel)

sources.name: Orthodox Union (OU)
sources.description: Official OU kosher certification website with extensive resources

sources.name: OK Kosher Certification
sources.description: Official OK Kosher website with product search and information

sources.name: Star-K Kosher Certification
sources.description: Star-K official website with detailed kosher guides and articles

sources.name: Chicago Rabbinical Council (CRC)
sources.description: CRC kosher certification and Passover guide publisher

practical_applications: Identifying kosher symbols while grocery shopping
Understanding dairy, meat, and pareve classifications
Planning kosher meals based on certification codes
learning_objectives: Recognize the five major kosher certification symbols
Understand what D, P, M, and Fish codes mean
Learn how kosher supervision actually works
Know when to consult a rabbi about kosher questions
chinese_cultural_notes: Comparison made to traditional Chinese workshop culture and neighborhood trust systems
Parallel drawn to traditional Chinese medicine ingredient standards
Reference to Confucian dietary refinement concepts
Note on similarity between kosher separation of meat/dairy and Chinese food compatibility concepts (相克)
recommended_follow_up: Article on specific kosher dietary laws
Guide to setting up a kosher kitchen
production_notes: Article commissioned to explain kosher certification symbols commonly encountered in supermarkets. Focuses on practical identification and understanding of major hechsherim. Includes Chinese cultural parallels comparing kosher supervision to traditional craftsmanship and food preparation standards.
